name: Pasta, Beef & Custard Pie
servings: prep time: 10 minutes | cook time:45 minutes |serves 8 to 12

ingredients.item: each dried Greek oregano, crumbled, and ground cinnamon
macaroni pastitsio No. 2 or 3, penne, or ziti
large egg plus 2 large egg yolks
3/4 cup grated kefalotyri or Parmesan cheese

method: In a large skillet, combine the 1/4 cup olive oil, onions, and garlic. Sauté over high heat until the onions soften but do not color, 4 minutes. Add the beef and sauté, breaking up clumps with a wooden spoon, until the meat is no longer pink, 3 minutes. Continue cooking until the meat releases its juices, 2 to 3 minutes longer. Drain in a colander set over a bowl. Return the meat to the pan. Let the juices settle for 5 minutes, then skim the surface fat and return the juices to the pan. Add the tomato paste, honey, bay leaves, oregano, cinnamon, allspice, 1/2 teaspoon of the nutmeg, 1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper. Simmer gently, stirring occasionally, until all the liquid has been absorbed and the meat is moist but not soupy, about 10 minutes. Remove the bay leaves.

In a large pot of salted boiling water, cook the pasta until al dente, according to package directions. Drain, rinse with cold water, drain again, then coat with the 1 tablespoon olive oil to prevent sticking.

Put the béchamel in a large bowl and whisk until smooth. Whisk in the egg, egg yolks, cheese, 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg, and salt to taste to make a custard. Preheat the oven to 425°F. Mix the pasta with 1 cup of the custard. Spread half the pasta into an even layer on the bottom of a 13-by-10-by-21/2-inch baking dish. Top with all the meat, pressing it into an even layer with the back of a wooden spoon. Top with the remaining pasta. Dollop the remaining custard on top, then spread gently. Place the dish on a baking sheet and bake until the pastitsio is well browned and set yet still quivery, about 45 minutes. Let cool for at least 45 minutes before slicing. Serve with tomato sauce underneath each piece, if desired.
